title: A noise from my childhood I still hear
summary: Describe a sound from your childhood and explain what it makes you feel.
topic: A noise from my childhood I still hear
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a noise from your childhood that you still remember. Describe the sound clearly and say where it came from. Use past simple and past continuous. Explain what you feel when you hear a similar sound today.
hintStarters: One sound from my childhood that I still remember is…
I used to hear it every time…
When I hear something similar today, I…
sampleText: One sound from my childhood that I still remember is the bell of the old tram in Lisbon. I grew up near the city centre, and when I was young, the trams ran every morning on the street below our apartment. The bell rang out just before the tram turned the corner. It was a short, clear sound — two rings, then a pause. I used to wake up to that sound every school day. While my mother was making breakfast in the kitchen, the tram bell would ring and I knew it was time to get up. The sound was not loud, but it was very familiar. When the trams stopped on our line for repairs one year, I noticed how quiet the mornings felt. I actually missed the noise. Now I live in a different city, but when I visit Lisbon and hear a tram bell in the distance, it takes me straight back to that small bedroom and those slow, warm mornings. It is a faint sound, but it carries a lot of memory. I think that is the strange thing about childhood sounds — they are small, but they hold your whole world inside them.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chuông của chiếc xe điện cũ
những chuyến xe điện chạy mỗi buổi sáng
hai tiếng chuông, rồi một khoảng lặng
thức dậy với âm thanh đó
mẹ tôi đang làm bữa sáng
tôi thực sự nhớ tiếng ồn đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Used to + infinitive
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'used to + động từ nguyên mẫu' để diễn tả thói quen hoặc tình huống trong quá khứ không còn xảy ra nữa (I used to wake up to that sound).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'used to + infinitive' for habits or situations that were true in the past but are no longer true ('I used to wake up to that sound every school day').

sampleTranslationVi: Một âm thanh từ thời thơ ấu mà tôi vẫn còn nhớ là tiếng chuông của chiếc xe điện cũ ở Lisbon. Tôi lớn lên gần trung tâm thành phố, và khi còn nhỏ, những chuyến xe điện chạy mỗi buổi sáng trên con phố bên dưới căn hộ của chúng tôi. Tiếng chuông vang lên ngay trước khi xe điện quẹo góc. Đó là một âm thanh ngắn, rõ ràng — hai tiếng chuông, rồi một khoảng lặng. Tôi thường thức dậy với âm thanh đó mỗi ngày đi học. Trong khi mẹ tôi đang làm bữa sáng trong bếp, tiếng chuông xe điện sẽ vang lên và tôi biết đã đến giờ dậy. Âm thanh không lớn, nhưng rất quen thuộc. Khi các chuyến xe điện trên đường của chúng tôi ngừng hoạt động một năm để sửa chữa, tôi nhận ra những buổi sáng yên tĩnh như thế nào. Tôi thực sự nhớ tiếng ồn đó. Bây giờ tôi sống ở một thành phố khác, nhưng khi về thăm Lisbon và nghe tiếng chuông xe điện ở phía xa, nó đưa tôi thẳng trở về căn phòng nhỏ đó và những buổi sáng chậm rãi, ấm áp. Đó là một âm thanh mờ nhạt, nhưng nó mang theo nhiều ký ức. Tôi nghĩ đó là điều kỳ lạ về những âm thanh thời thơ ấu — chúng nhỏ, nhưng chúng chứa đựng cả thế giới của bạn bên trong.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple for 'ring'.
options: The bell rang out just before the tram turned the corner.
The bell ringed out just before the tram turned the corner.
The bell was ringing out just before the tram turns the corner.
The bell has rung out just before the tram turned the corner.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'ring'?

options: I used to wake up to that sound every school day.
I used to woke up to that sound every school day.
I use to wake up to that sound every school day.
I was used to wake up to that sound every school day.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Used to' is followed by the base infinitive ('wake up').
mcQuestions.hint: After 'used to', use the base form of the verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct background description using past continuous.
options: While my mother was making breakfast, the tram bell rang.
While my mother made breakfast, the tram bell was ringing.
While my mother is making breakfast, the tram bell rang.
While my mother was making breakfast, the tram bell was rung.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous ('was making') for background; past simple ('rang') for the event.
mcQuestions.hint: Background action = past continuous; sudden event = past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means a sound that is heard far away?
options: in the distance
in the far
at the distance
from distant
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In the distance' is the standard phrase for something heard or seen far away.
mcQuestions.hint: Which is the fixed phrase meaning 'far away'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the word that means a sound is soft and hard to hear clearly.
options: faint
faint-hearted
loud
sharp
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Faint' describes a sound that is weak or barely audible.
mcQuestions.hint: Which adjective means 'not loud, difficult to hear'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means something makes you remember the past strongly?
options: it takes me back
it brings me forward
it gives me back
it pulls me past
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Take someone back' means to make someone remember or feel like they are in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb means 'to make you remember the past'?

options: The sound was very familiar.
The sound was very familiarly.
The sound was very familiarity.
The sound was very familiared.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Familiar' is an adjective. It follows the verb 'be' without any change.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about missing a sound.
options: I actually missed the noise.
I actually was missing the noise.
I actually miss the noise when I was there.
I actually missed to the noise.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'missed' fits the story about a past event.
mcQuestions.hint: The story is in the past — use past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence structure best introduces the main topic?
options: One sound from my childhood that I still remember is the tram bell.
One sound from my childhood what I still remember is the tram bell.
One sound of my childhood that I still remember is the tram bell.
One sound from my childhood which I still remembers is the tram bell.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'That' is the correct relative pronoun here, and the verb is 'remember' (not 'remembers') because the subject is 'I'.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'that' as the relative pronoun and match the verb to the subject 'I'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fills the gap? 'It was a short, ___ sound — two rings, then a pause.'
options: clear
cleaning
clearly
clarity
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Clear' is an adjective that describes the quality of the sound.
mcQuestions.hint: Which form of the word is an adjective?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural way to end a memory paragraph.
options: I think that is the strange thing about childhood sounds — they are small, but they hold your whole world inside them.
In conclusion, childhood sounds are very important and meaningful for all people.
To summarize, sounds from the past are good memories to have.
Therefore, we should always remember the sounds of our childhood years.
mcQuestions.explanation: The first option uses natural, reflective language without formulaic phrases.
mcQuestions.hint: Avoid 'In conclusion' and 'To summarize' — they sound too formal for personal writing.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think certain sounds can bring back memories so strongly? Is there a difference between how sounds and images make you feel?
